title: Patrol force-loop workaround masks stalled WAYMODE_MAGIC (M2.5)
suspect: src/game/chrlv.c:11328 (per-tick modelSetAnimLooping force -- applies walk root-motion even in WAYMODE_MAGIC)

history.note: First trace-grade machine evidence + §4.1 statement (FID-0054 investigation, 2026-07-11). RETAIL keeps unseen patrolling guards frozen at their pad in WAYMODE_MAGIC, walking them virtually and teleporting pad-to-pad on virtual segment completion (dam_combat_guard6 stock oracle: patrol guards chr 2/40/45 stationary 96-99.7% of pre-onset ticks; position steps 100-1900u at warp instants; recently-drawn guards walk normally at 2.8u/t). PORT walks all 8 Dam patrollers smoothly on every tick (892/892 observed, zero pauses): chrlv.c:11348 blocks magic ENTRY for rendered-room guards, and chrlv.c:11328's per-tick anim-loop force applies walk root-motion even while IN magic mode (live GE007_TRACE_MAGIC_TRAVEL: chr 41/42/43/45 mode=6 creeping 1-5u/t + immediate ground-Y snap at tick 1). Effect: every patrolling guard's position diverges from retail from sim tick 1 under IDENTICAL initial state (spawn byte-identical; up to ~640u within 22s at equal ages; up to 2000u observed cross-side) -- this is FID-0054's Layer B and the upstream driver of the FID-0063 §9.2 perception-class RNG residual, so it now GATES D5 call-for-call RNG parity. Priority P2->P1 accordingly. Faithful fix shape (retail ONSCREEN semantics + un-forced anim looping + retail magic entry/exit) must land with FID-0012/M2.3 Phase C per the BACKLOG M2.5 sequencing note; regression bar = dam_combat_guard6_agealign both-sides capture (recipe layer removed) with the 8-guard patrol set matching stock pause/warp profiles.
